nodes.description: The immune system mistakenly attacks the adrenal cortex, leading to destruction of adrenal tissue and decreased hormone production. Lymphocytic infiltration of zona glomerulosa and zona fasciculata causes progressive loss of steroidogenic cells.

biological_processes: antigen processing and presentation of peptide antigen via MHC class II

evidence.reference_title: Autoimmune Addison's disease - An update on pathogenesis.
evidence.snippet: Autoimmunity against the adrenal cortex is the leading cause of Addison's disease in industrialized countries... The immune-mediated attack on adrenocortical cells cripples their ability to synthesize vital steroid hormones.
In most cases, autoimmune destruction of the adrenal cortex is the cause of Addison's disease.
Hypoadrenocorticism typically results from immune-mediated destruction of all adrenocortical layers, resulting in deficiencies of min-eralocorticoids (aldosterone) and glucocorticoids (cortisol).

evidence.reference_title: Mistaken Identity: The Role of Autoantibodies in Endocrine Disease.
evidence.snippet: The immune system mistakenly attacks one's own cells, as if they were foreign, which typically results in endocrine gland hypofunction and inadequate hormone production... conditions such as Addison disease are encountered less frequently.

meta.association: Transcription factor controlling B- and T-cell differentiation
Master regulator of MHC class II expression
GWAS-implicated locus linked to antigen presentation and autophagy
Immune checkpoint regulator; risk variants reduce T-cell inhibition

evidence.reference_title: CTLA-4 as a genetic determinant in autoimmune Addison's disease.
evidence.snippet: The G-allele of SNP rs231775 in CTLA-4 is associated with AAD in Norwegian patients (odds ratio (OR)=1.35 (confidence interval (CI) 1.10-1.66), P=0.004)

meta.association: Inflammasome component; variants implicate inflammatory pathways
Tyrosine phosphatase modulating T-cell receptor signaling

evidence.reference_title: Mutation screening of PTPN22: association of the 1858T-allele with Addison's disease.
evidence.snippet: the 1858T-allele is a PTPN22 genetic susceptibility factor for autoimmune AD

nodes.description: Multiple genetic risk loci contribute to autoimmune Addison's disease susceptibility, including HLA class II haplotypes and immune regulatory genes. HLA-DR3-DQ2 and HLA-DR4-DQ8 alter antigen presentation, while non-HLA genes affect T-cell regulation, checkpoint signaling, and inflammatory responses.

biological_processes: negative regulation of T cell receptor signaling pathway

nodes.description: Insufficient aldosterone affects the balance of sodium and potassium in the body, leading to dehydration and low blood pressure. Impaired sodium retention and potassium excretion in renal tubules cause electrolyte disturbances.

evidence.reference_title: Disorders of the adrenal cortex: their effects on electrolyte metabolism.
evidence.snippet: On the other hand, impaired secretion, as in Addison's disease or in congenital deficiencies of other steroid-synthesizing enzymes, leads to hypotension, sodium loss with hypovolaemia, and hyperkalaemia.

evidence.reference_title: An Addison disease revealed with a serious hyponatremia.
evidence.snippet: Addison's disease is characterized by the destruction of the adrenal cortex. Autoimmune adrenalitis is the main cause of adrenal insufficiency. Treatment involves normalisation of sodium concentration and corticosteroids replacement.

nodes.description: Lack of cortisol impacts the body's ability to respond to stress and maintain various metabolic functions. Impaired gluconeogenesis, decreased vascular tone, and altered immune responses result from cortisol deficiency.

evidence.snippet: Addison's disease, or primary adrenal insufficiency, results in glucocorticoid and mineralocorticoid deficiency... chronic primary adrenal insufficiency presents with a more insidious history of malaise, anorexia, diarrhea, weight loss... hypoglycemia characterize acute adrenal crisis.

evidence.reference_title: Factors impacting on the action of glucocorticoids in patients receiving glucocorticoid therapy.
evidence.snippet: Adrenal insufficiency (AI) is a rare, life-threatening disorder characterized by insufficient production of corticosteroid hormones... Conventional treatment of both primary and secondary adrenal insufficiencies involves lifelong glucocorticoid replacement therapy.

evidence.reference_title: Addison's disease presenting as loss of weight without pigmentation.
evidence.snippet: Addison's disease presenting as loss of weight without pigmentation.
Addison's disease is a relatively rare endocrine condition resulting from adrenal insufficiency due to various causes. Weight loss is a common feature.
We report the case within a 22-year-old patient, initially seen because of fatigue, weight loss and discoloration of the skin.

evidence.reference_title: Addison's disease symptoms--a cross sectional study in urban South Africa.
evidence.snippet: Hyperpigmentation was observed in 76%, nausea and vomiting occurred in more than 40%, and weight loss was noted in 25%.
